question: A group of 4 from a gym: two want high protein, one is vegetarian, one has a nut allergy. They want a post‑workout meal.

history.speaker: client
history.text: A group of 4 from a gym: two want high protein, one is vegetarian, one has a nut allergy. They want a post‑workout meal.

history.speaker: cashier
history.text: Sorry, I didn't catch that clearly. Could you repeat your order?

history.speaker: client
history.text: Two need high protein, one is vegetarian, one has a nut allergy—this group of four wants a post-workout meal.

history.speaker: cashier
history.text: The group can order the following meals to meet all requirements: 1. Double Quarter Pounder with Cheese (48g protein) → For one high-protein person (no nuts listed in allergens). 2. 10pc Chicken McNuggets (25g protein) → For the second high-protein person (no nuts listed in allergens). 3. McVeggie (10.2g protein) → For the vegetarian person (vegetarian option, no meat). 4. Side Salad (1g protein) → For the nut allergy person (no allergens listed, safe for nut allergy). ### Why this works: - High protein (2 people): Double Quarter Pounder (48g) and 10pc Chicken McNuggets (25g) are both high-protein options with no nuts in their allergens. - Vegetarian (1 person): McVeggie is explicitly vegetarian (no meat) and meets dietary needs. - Nut allergy (1 person): Side Salad has no allergens listed, making it safe for nut allergy. - All other items avoid nuts: None of the menu items contain nuts except the specified "Sundae (Chocolate Brownie)" and "Chocolate Brownie" (which are excluded here). This combination ensures all group members have safe, appropriate meals while prioritizing post-workout protein needs.

history.speaker: judge

- Keep replies to 2–3 sentences max, like a real drive-through.
